title: /kernel
summary_text: From these files in this repo, the minimum `/kernel` bootstrap requirements are: 1. Required voices - Normal shape: `codex` + `glm` + one specialist (`gemini-cli` or `cursor-cli` or `copilot-cli`). - `codex` is mandatory as sovereign. - `Claude` is explicitly not a bootstrap prerequisite. 2. Minimum lane count - At least 6 concurrent active lanes (`Bootstrap target: 6+ lanes (minimum 6)`). - All 6+ lanes must be materially distinct and currently active, not just planned/pending/failed. 3. When `BLOCKED` is allowed - `BLOCKED` is allowed only when neither the normal shape nor degraded-allowed shape can be established. - A degraded-allowed shape is only valid after `glm` has failed twice in the same run: `codex + specialist + specialist`. - If environment cannot sustain the 6+ active-lane minimum, fail closed (`BLOCKED`), do not fall below minimum. 4. Mandatory receipt requirements - Before first acknowledgement, write bootstrap receipt: `bash scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh write <lane_count> <providers_csv> <normal|degraded-allowed>` - Also set/export these env vars before first acknowledgement: - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_ACTIVE_MODELS_CSV` - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_MANIFEST_LANE_COUNT` - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_AGENT_LABELS=true` - `KERNEL_BOOTSTRAP_SUBAGENT_LABELS=true` - Receipt should be updated again if Kernel mode changes.
